revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24618454543|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小松神社 (交野市)]]
{{infobox religious building | name = Komatsu Shrine (小松神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Hoshidamyokengu.jpg | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Katano|en|Katano|qid=Q819646|lt=Katano|1=Katano}} {{ill|Hoshida (Katano)|ja|星田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshida (Katano)|1=Hoshida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512963}} 9-chome, 60-1 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.759472|N|135.678583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} | shrine_Rank = | location = {{ill|Katano|qid=Q819646|1=Katano}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Hoshida Myōkengū'''|星田妙見宮}}, also known as {{nihongo|'''Komatsu Shrine'''|小松神社}}, is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hoshida (Katano)|ja|星田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshida (Katano)|1=Hoshida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512963}}, {{ill|Katano|en|Katano|qid=Q819646|lt=Katano|1=Katano}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a notable sacred site retaining remnants of {{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|qid=Q11587880|lt=Iwakura|1=Iwakura rock}} worship within the {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|qid=Q996259|lt=Ikoma Mountains|1=Mount Ikoma}} range.
== Deities ==
* Main deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇産霊大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}
Historically, these deities were associated with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|qid=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|1=Myōken}} in {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}} and {{ill|Taioshin Sen|ja|太上神仙鎮宅霊符神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taioshin Sen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|qid=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}} until the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}.
== History ==
According to legend, in the {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|qid=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin era|1=Kōnin (era)}} (810–823), {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} visited the {{ill|Katano|en|Katano|qid=Q819646|lt=Katano|1=Katano}} region and entered the Lion Cave (獅子窟) at {{ill|Shishikutsu-dera|ja|獅子窟寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishikutsu-dera|1=Shishikutsu-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572208}}, where he chanted secret prayers. Seven celestial stars, interpreted as the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|qid=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper|1=Big Dipper}}, descended and scattered across three locations in Katano. One of these locations became the site of Komatsu Shrine.
Kūkai reportedly established the shrine, naming it "Sankō Seigan Shōshin Myōken" and venerating the fallen star as a deity.<ref name="shrine-origin">[https://www.hoshida-myoken.com/%E3%81%94%E7%94%B1%E7%B7%92/ Official website: Shrine origins]</ref>
During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the shrine was known as "Shinzenji" (神禅寺) and appeared in historical documents such as those from {{ill|Kongō-ji|ja|金剛寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji|1=Kongō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11646519}} in {{ill|Kawachinagano|en|Kawachinagano|qid=Q819572|lt=Kawachinagano|1=Kawachinagano}}. Records such as the {{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|qid=Q1145735|lt=Enpō era|1=Enpō}} (1678) "Tōwada Genealogy" indicate the shrine served as a bettō (clerical caretaker) shrine over generations.<ref name="shrine-origin"/>
The shrine has been associated with {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|qid=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata|1=Qixi Festival}} rituals since ancient times, as noted in texts like the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図絵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1801), which describes its sacred {{ill|iwakura|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iwakura|qq=}} as "Orimeishi" (織女石), dedicated to {{ill|Zhinü|en|Zhinü|qid=Q2122828|lt=Orihime|1=Zhinü}}. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was also mentioned in {{ill|Kaibara Ekken|en|Kaibara Ekken|qid=Q1721396|lt=Kaibara Ekken|1=Kaibara Ekken}}'s travelogue, ''Nanyū Kikō''.<ref name="shrine-origin"/>
In 2016, the shrine celebrated its 1200th anniversary with commemorative events featuring local exhibits, performances, and a lecture by comet hunter {{ill|Tsuruhiko Kiyota|ja|木内鶴彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuruhiko Kiyota|1=Tsuruhiko Kiuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11518178}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.katano.osaka.jp/docs/2016062200010/file_contents/tokushu1.pdf|title=Katano City Public Relations, July 2016|accessdate=2018-12-14}}</ref>
The shrine is also renowned for its cherry blossoms and has been designated as "Myōken no Kansakura," one of Katano's Eight Scenic Spots.<ref name="tourism-association">[https://katano-kanko.com/%E6%98%9F%E7%94%B0%E5%A6%99%E8%A6%8B%E5%AE%AE/ Katano City Tourism Association: Hoshida Myōken Shrine]</ref>
== Grounds ==
* ''Eikyōseki'' - Sacred rock and main deity. Also known as ''Orimeishi'', reflecting ancient {{ill|iwakura|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iwakura|qq=}} worship. * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tōryū Falls * Ema Hall * Assembly Hall * Jizō Hall
=== Auxiliary Shrines ===
* {{ill|Chintaku Shrine|ja|鎮宅社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579034|1=Chintaku Shrine}} – Dedicated to {{ill|Taioshin Sen Chintaku Reifu no Kami|ja|太上神仙鎮宅霊符神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taioshin Sen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135583892}} * Sanpō Kōjin Shrine – Dedicated to {{ill|Sanpō Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpō Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbo Kojin|qid=Q13577821}} * Toyotomi Inari Shrine – Dedicated to {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukemochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 保食神|1=Ukemochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2280323}} * Ryūō Shrine – Dedicated to Toyotama Ryūō and Toyosei Ryūō * Seiryū Shrine – Dedicated to Seiryū Daimyōjin * Ryūjin Shrine – Dedicated to Konjiki Ryūō * Shimo-sha Inari Shrine – Dedicated to {{ill|Inari Ōkami|ja|倉稲魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080728}} * Sorei Shrine – Dedicated to ancestral spirits of the shrine's priests * Kōshin Shrine
== Festivals ==
* {{ill|February 8|en|February 8|qid=Q2323|lt=February 8|1=February 8}} – {{ill|Hoshi Matsuri|ja|星祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshi Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=Star Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512767}} * {{ill|July 7|en|July 7|qid=Q2694|lt=July 7|1=July 7}} – {{ill|Tanabata|ja|七夕祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 七夕|1=Qixi Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13423122}} * {{ill|July 23|en|July 23|qid=Q2722|lt=July 23|1=July 23}} – Hoshi Furi Matsuri (Starfall Festival)
== location ==
* 9-60-1 Hoshida, Katano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan
== Access ==
* Approx. 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Hoshida Station|en|Hoshida Station|qid=Q856066|lt=Hoshida Station|1=Hoshida Station}} on the {{ill|JR Gakkentoshi Line|ja|学研都市線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Gakkentoshi Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 片町線|1=Katamachi Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1073379}}. * Approx. 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kisaichi Station|en|Kisaichi Station|qid=Q10858384|lt=Kisaichi Station|1=Kisaichi Station}} on the {{ill|Keihan Katano Line|en|Keihan Katano Line|qid=Q126015|lt=Keihan Katano Line|1=Keihan Katano Line}}. * From Hoshida Station or {{ill|Katanoshi Station|en|Katanoshi Station|qid=Q6375107|lt=Katano-shi Station|1=Katanoshi Station}}, take {{ill|Keihan Bus|en|Keihan Bus|qid=Q11375813|lt=Keihan Bus|1=Keihan Bus}} routes 17 or 17A and disembark at "Myōkenguchi" stop. The shrine's first torii gate is a 15-minute walk away.
